subject: Cloud Computing
title: The Cost of Cloud Lock-In
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Cost of Cloud Lock-In.

hook.text: A company built everything on AWS Lambda and DynamoDB. Switching providers would cost millions and years.

buildup.text: Lock-in happens when you use proprietary services that don't exist elsewhere. Your code becomes tied to one provider.

discovery.text: Multi-cloud avoids lock-in but adds complexity. Running two clouds is more than twice the operational burden.

twist.text: Some lock-in is intentional. Providers make their unique services irresistibly easy and deeply integrated.

climax.text: The real cost of lock-in isn't the bill — it's lost leverage. You can't threaten to leave if leaving is impossible.

punchline.text: Lock-in starts with convenience. It ends with dependency.

quiz.question: What is the real economic cost of cloud lock-in?
options: Lost negotiating power because switching providers is too costly
Cloud providers charge a penalty fee for being locked in
Lock-in makes cloud services run slower over time
